title: 10 best herbal teas for heart pain

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Hawthorn tea's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to cardiovascular disease and heart pain.

Vasodilatory effects: The flavonoids present in hawthorn tea help relax blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure, which can alleviate symptoms of angina and other forms of heart pain.

Cardioprotective compounds: Hawthorn tea contains oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) and flavonoids that have been shown to strengthen the heart muscle, improve circulation, and reduce the risk of arrhythmias and other cardiac issues.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna, also known as hawthorn, teas helps with heart pain because of its unique properties that support cardiovascular health. The bioactive compounds in hawthorn tea, such as flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, have been shown to improve blood flow, lower blood pressure, and enhance the heart's pumping efficiency. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, hawthorn tea helps to alleviate symptoms of heart pain and promote overall cardiovascular well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for heart health issues.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help alleviate heart pain by reducing inflammation and improving blood vessel function, which can help to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of heart disease.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, particularly 2,3-bisnor-2-dihydroaescin, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the heart from damage and reduce symptoms of heart pain.

Procyanidins: Procyanidins, a type of flavonoid, have been found to have a positive effect on cardiovascular health by improving blood vessel function, reducing inflammation, and preventing the formation of blood clots, all of which can contribute to heart pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make teas due to their high content of flavonoids and polyphenols, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate heart pain.
Fruits: Used to make teas due to their high content of flavonoids, particularly quercetin and epicatechin, which have been shown to have cardiovascular benefits and may help reduce heart pain.
Buds: Used to make teas due to their high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help improve cardiovascular health and reduce heart pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves and berries from the crataegus monogyna tree in late summer or early autumn.
Dry the harvested leaves and berries in a warm dark place for at least 2 weeks.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried leaves and 1 tablespoon of dried berries with 1 liter of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes and then strain it using a tea infuser or cheesecloth.
Serve the tea warm or chilled and enjoy it at a dosage of 1 cup per day.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to heart pain by improving cardiovascular health.
Antiplatelet Activity: The tea's antiplatelet compounds inhibit platelet aggregation, reducing the risk of blood clot formation, which can cause heart attacks and angina (heart pain).

Cardioprotective Flavonoids: Ginkgo biloba contains flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, that have been shown to reduce blood pressure, improve blood flow, and protect against cardiovascular damage, all of which can help alleviate heart pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, teas helps with heart pain because of its ability to improve blood flow to the heart. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba extract help to dilate blood vessels, reducing inflammation and improving oxygenation of the heart. This increased blood flow can help to alleviate symptoms of angina, such as chest pain and shortness of breath, making it a popular natural remedy for heart health. Regular consumption may also help to reduce blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, help with heart pain by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the heart, thereby alleviating conditions like angina.

Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone is believed to help with heart pain by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to heart conditions like atherosclerosis.

Ginkgolides: These sesquiterpenes, particularly ginkgolide A and B, help with heart pain by inhibiting platelet aggregation and improving blood flow, which can help prevent conditions like heart attack and stroke.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the heart.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are also used for their antioxidant properties, which help to protect the heart from damage caused by free radicals and promote overall cardiovascular health.
Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the heart, making it a popular choice for heart pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in late summer or early fall season.
Rinse the leaves with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the leaves in a warm place for 2-3 days or use a food dehydrator at 95°f for 6 hours.
Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool to room temperature before serving.

remedy_properties: Lavender oil's vasodilatory effect: English lavender tea has been shown to dilate blood vessels, which can help reduce blood pressure and alleviate angina (chest pain) caused by coronary artery disease.
Anxiolytic properties: The calming effects of lavender tea may help reduce anxiety and stress levels, which are common comorbidities with heart conditions and can exacerbate symptoms of heart pain.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lavender tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which may help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and alleviate symptoms of heart pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, teas helps with heart pain because of its calming and soothing properties. The tea's active compounds, linalool and linalyl acetate, have a relaxing effect on the mind and body, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate heart pain. By promoting relaxation and reducing tension, Lavandula angustifolia tea may help alleviate heart pain and promote a sense of calm and well-being. Its natural properties make it a potential complementary remedy for heart health.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate heart pain by reducing inflammation in the cardiovascular system.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the heart from damage caused by free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, thereby alleviating heart pain.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has a sedative effect, which can help reduce anxiety and stress that can contribute to heart pain, promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate heart pain.
Flowers: They are rich in linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a calming effect and may help reduce stress and anxiety that can contribute to heart pain.
Stems: They contain a combination of flavonoids and other compounds that may help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, reducing heart pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 to 30 dried lavandula angustifolia flowers to ensure a sufficient tea quantity.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve to remove loose plant material.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature within 10 to 15 minutes.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedy_properties: Cardioprotective flavonoids: Valerian tea contains a range of flavonoids, such as isovaltrate and valerin, which have been shown to protect against cardiovascular disease by reducing inflammation, improving blood flow, and preventing platelet aggregation.

Anti-inflammatory sesquiterpenes: The sesquiterpenes in valerian tea, particularly valerenic acid, have potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the risk of heart damage and alleviate symptoms of angina pectoris (chest pain) by decreasing inflammation in blood vessels.

Cardiac glycoside-like compounds: Valerian tea contains cardiac glycoside-like compounds that can help regulate heartbeat and improve blood flow to the heart, which may be beneficial for individuals with arrhythmias or congestive heart failure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, teas helps with heart pain because of its natural ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. The tea's active compounds, valerenic acid and valeranone, have been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate heart pain by reducing stress and anxiety. Additionally, valerian tea has been found to have a calming effect on the heart, slowing down heart rate and promoting a sense of calm, which can help provide relief from heart pain.
remedy_constituents: Isovaleric acid: Isovaleric acid, a short-chain fatty acid, helps reduce inflammation and anxiety, which can contribute to heart pain.
Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid, a sesquiterpene, acts as a GABA receptor agonist, promoting relaxation and reducing stress, which can alleviate heart pain.
Valeranone: Valeranone, a valerenic acid-derived compound, has sedative and anxiolytic properties, helping to calm the nervous system and ease heart pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of valerenic acid, which has a sedative effect that can help alleviate heart pain and anxiety.
Flowers: They contain valerenic acid and other compounds that contribute to the plant's sedative and relaxing properties, making them suitable for heart pain relief.
Stems: Although less commonly used, the stems of Valeriana officinalis contain some of the same compounds as the roots and flowers, which can be used to make teas for heart pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 ounces of fresh valeriana officinalis roots and rinse them thoroughly with filtered water.
Chop the roots into smaller pieces using a sharp knife and weigh them for accurate dosages.
Combine 1 tablespoon of the chopped roots with 1 quart of boiling water and let it steep.
Allow the tea to infuse for 5-7 minutes before straining it using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Store the cooled tea in a glass container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Hyperforin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, alleviating symptoms of heart pain.

Hypotensive effect: The active compounds in St John's Wort tea, including hypericin and flavonoids, have a hypotensive effect, meaning they can help lower blood pressure, which is often a contributing factor to heart pain and chest discomfort.

Antiarrhythmic properties: St John's Wort tea has been found to have antiarrhythmic effects, meaning it can help regulate abnormal heart rhythms and reduce the risk of arrhythmias, which can be a symptom of heart pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort, teas helps with heart pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in St. John's Wort, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the heart, which can help alleviate chest pain and other cardiovascular symptoms. Additionally, St. John's Wort has been traditionally used to promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can also contribute to its heart pain-relieving properties.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: A phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with heart pain.
N-feruloylserotonin: A phenolic amide that has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help relieve pain and reduce inflammation in the heart.

Quercetin: A flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with heart pain, as well as improve cardiovascular health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain bioactive compounds like hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate heart pain.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, the flowers have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety-related heart pain.
Stems: The stems contain a higher concentration of hypericin and hyperforin, making them a popular choice for teas aimed at reducing inflammation and promoting cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 tablespoons of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers or 1 teaspoon of dried flowers for a single serving.
Dry the flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 1 hour.
Steep the dried flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids after 2-3 minutes of steeping.
Store the tea in a sealed glass container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of melissa officinalis leaves from mature plants in the morning.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm place for 2-3 hours or use a food dehydrator at 95f for 1-2 hours.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried melissa officinalis leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea immediately using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the herb.
Serve the tea warm or at room temperature and enjoy within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedy_recipe: Select 2-3 teaspoons of dried camellia sinensis leaves for every 8 ounces of water.
Rinse the leaves in cold running water for 10 seconds to remove impurities and dust.
Heat 8 ounces of filtered water to 200-212°f in a teapot for 5 minutes.
Steep the camellia sinensis leaves in the heated water for 3-5 minutes or until desired strength.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or tea strainer to remove the leaves.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which can help reduce inflammation in the body and alleviate heart pain by decreasing swelling and irritation in blood vessels.

Strain the taraxacum officinale tea mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 fresh centella asiatica leaves and stems from a clean and pesticide-free area.
Rinse the centella asiatica leaves and stems with cold water for 5 minutes to remove impurities.
Chop the centella asiatica leaves and stems into small pieces and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest gentiana lutea roots dig freshly from a trusted source or store-bought dried variety.

Combine measured gentiana lutea root with boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes to allow active compounds to seep into the liquid.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in amounts of one teaspoon for every 8 ounces of boiling water.

Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids.
Add honey to taste if desired and let it steep for an additional minute before serving.
Drink the warm tea three times daily as needed up to six weeks to alleviate heart pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with heart pain because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the cardiovascular system. The mucilage in marshmallow root has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to heart pain. Additionally, the flavonoids present in marshmallow tea have been shown to improve blood flow and lower blood pressure, further alleviating symptoms of heart pain. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, marshmallow teas offer a natural solution for those experiencing heart discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow root has anti-inflammatory compounds like mucilage, which can help reduce inflammation and ease chest pain associated with heart conditions.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in marshmallow tea can help protect the heart from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and improving overall cardiovascular health.

Cardioprotective effects: Marshmallow root has been shown to have cardioprotective effects, potentially helping to lower blood pressure, improve blood lipid profiles, and reduce cardiac fibrosis (scarring) that can contribute to heart pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soothe and calm inflammation in the heart.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain flavonoids and saponins that help to reduce blood pressure and alleviate angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried marshmallow root from trusted sources, measuring two to three teaspoons per cup.
Combine the herb with boiling water in a teapot or infuser, steeping for five to seven minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids immediately.
Add honey or lemon to taste if desired, adjusting sweetness and flavor as needed before serving.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consumption, recommending sipping slowly to fully experience benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain teas helps with heart pain because it possesses potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the cardiovascular system. The leaves of the plantain plant contain a compound called mucilage, which forms a protective barrier on the lining of blood vessels, reducing inflammation and swelling caused by conditions such as angina or coronary artery disease. By calming the inflammatory response, plantain tea helps to alleviate chest pain, improve circulation, and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Plantain tea's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to heart pain by reducing blood flow and damaging cardiovascular tissues.

Cardioprotective Compounds: The plantain tea contains cardio-protective flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to prevent cardiac arrhythmias, lower blood pressure, and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, all of which can help alleviate heart pain.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Plantain tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the body, which is a common underlying cause of heart pain (angina) by reducing the constriction of coronary arteries and improving blood flow to the heart.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in starch and fiber, roots help to soothe and calm the cardiovascular system, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Barks: Containing compounds like tannins and phenolics, barks have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in relieving heart pain and promoting circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 tablespoons of dried plantago major leaves from a trusted source.
Rinse the dried leaves with boiling water for 10-15 seconds to awaken their medicinal properties.
Steep the plantago major leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release their bioactive compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids retaining only the liquid.
Store the prepared tea in an airtight container and consume within 24 hours at a dosage of 1/2 cup three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mountain hydrangea teas helps with heart pain because it contains flavonoids, antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is often a contributing factor to heart pain. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, allowing for increased oxygen flow to the heart. Additionally, mountain hydrangea has been shown to help lower triglyceride levels and cholesterol, further reducing the risk of heart disease and alleviating symptoms of heart pain.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Mountain hydrangea tea contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and improve circulation, relieving chest pain caused by coronary artery disease.

Phenolic acids: The phenolic acids present in mountain hydrangea tea have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory effects, helping to dilate blood vessels and reduce blood pressure, which can alleviate symptoms of angina pectoris (heart pain).

Saponins: Saponins, a type of glycoside found in mountain hydrangea tea, have been demonstrated to possess anti-platelet aggregation properties, inhibiting the formation of blood clots that can contribute to heart attacks and stroke.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, the roots help to reduce inflammation and ease cardiovascular discomfort.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, the barks have antioxidant properties that protect against heart damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried hydrangea serrata leaves in the spring or fall when they are most potent.
Measure out one tablespoon of dried leaves or two tablespoons of fresh leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release their bioactive compounds and flavonoids.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot to remove solids and sediment.
Enjoy the warm, soothing tea within 30 minutes of preparation, ideally one to two cups daily for optimal benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregon grape teas helps with heart pain because it contains a unique combination of compounds that work to alleviate inflammation and improve circulation. The tea's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to heart pain and damage. Additionally, the tea's flavonoids and anthocyanins have been shown to strengthen blood vessels and improve overall cardiovascular health, helping to reduce symptoms of heart pain and promote a healthier heart.
remedy_properties: Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with heart pain, such as angina pectoris.

Berberine: This alkaloid has been shown to improve cardiovascular health by lowering cholesterol levels, blood pressure, and triglycerides, all of which can contribute to reduced risk of heart disease and related pain.

Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, potentially alleviating symptoms of heart pain such as chest tightness or discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing berberine, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, roots are effective in soothing heart pain by reducing inflammation.
Barks: Rich in saponins, barks have been traditionally used to reduce blood pressure and alleviate angina pectoris symptoms, making them a popular choice for medicinal teas.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried berberis aquifolium root bark or two teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Combine the herb with 250ml of boiling water for five minutes and then strain it.
Let the mixture steep for an additional seven minutes before adding honey if desired.
Bring the tea to a temperature that is comfortable for consumption usually between 60-70 degrees celsius.
Enjoy the tea once or twice daily for at least two weeks to potentially alleviate heart pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric teas helps with heart pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels. This can improve blood flow and lower blood pressure, both of which can alleviate chest pain and discomfort associated with heart conditions such as angina. Additionally, curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has been shown to improve cardiovascular health by reducing triglycerides and LDL cholesterol levels, further contributing to its potential benefits for heart health.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric's active compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and alleviate symptoms of angina and chest pain.

Cinnamon: Cinnamon, a common ingredient in turmeric teas, has been shown to improve blood flow and lower triglycerides, which can help reduce the risk of heart disease and alleviate symptoms of heart pain.
Ginger: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds have been found to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, which can help reduce chest pain and discomfort caused by reduced blood flow to the heart.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate heart pain.
Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant have been traditionally used to make teas that help to soothe heartburn and indigestion, which can contribute to heart pain.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water for 10 minutes then reduce heat to simmer.
Add one teaspoon of dried turmeric root powder to the boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes allowing the flavors to infuse and release.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth discarding solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly then drink within 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with heart pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the body that may be contributing to chest pain. The tea's ability to relax blood vessels and improve circulation may also help to alleviate angina symptoms by increasing oxygen delivery to the heart muscle. Additionally, ginger's antioxidant properties may help to protect against cell damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate heart conditions. Overall, ginger teas may provide a natural and effective way to manage heart pain and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains potent anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which can help reduce inflammation and ease chest pain associated with heart conditions.

Antioxidants: Ginger is rich in antioxidants, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, that can help protect the cardiovascular system from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, which may contribute to heart pain.

Vasodilatory properties: Ginger has been shown to exhibit vasodilatory effects, meaning it can help widen blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the risk of angina attacks and cardiac arrhythmias.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used to make medicinal teas as they contain active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate heart pain.
Roots: Roots of the ginger plant are used to prepare teas that can help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, relieving symptoms of heart pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger root from your garden or local market weighing approximately 200g.
Wash the ginger root thoroughly under cold running water to remove dirt and impurities.
Peel the ginger root using a vegetable peeler removing the outer layer exposing the inner flesh.
Slice the peeled ginger root into thin pieces measuring about 1-2 mm in thickness for brewing.
Steep one teaspoon of sliced ginger in boiling hot water for 5-7 minutes then strain and serve warm or cooled.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to heart pain that are treated with herbal teas are anxiety, stress, and high blood pressure. Herbs like hawthorn, passionflower, and lavender have been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and reduce symptoms of anxiety and stress. Other herbs such as dandelion root, garlic, and ginger may help lower blood pressure by reducing inflammation and improving circulation. These herbal teas can provide a natural alternative or complement to traditional treatments for these conditions.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for heart pain are natural remedies made from plants and herbs that help alleviate cardiovascular discomfort. These teas work by reducing inflammation, improving blood flow, and relaxing the heart. Examples include hawthorn, passionflower, and ginkgo biloba teas. Drinking these teas can help manage heart pain, improve overall well-being, and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, allowing individuals to lead healthier, more active lives and enjoy activities they love.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with heart pain is a blend of Hawthorn, Chamomile, and Lavender. Hawthorn is known to improve heart function and reduce inflammation, while Chamomile calms the nervous system and reduces anxiety. Lavender adds a soothing effect, reducing stress and promoting relaxation. Together, these teas work to alleviate heart pain by promoting a sense of calm, reducing inflammation, and improving heart function. This combination can be enjoyed as a warm, comforting brew to soothe the heart and promote overall well-being.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Plant foods for human nutrition (Dordrecht, Netherlands), herbal teas may help alleviate heart pain due to their potential to reduce the risk of heart disease. The study, which analyzed 145 research publications on herbal teas, found that certain plant species, such as hibiscus and yerba maté, may have beneficial effects on heart health. Although the study did not provide specific data on the reduction of heart pain, the observed associations between herbal tea consumption and heart disease risk suggest that regular consumption may contribute to a healthier cardiovascular system.
